mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, labor market weakness signals severe economic contraction, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with falling industrial production indicates a structural shift from manufacturing to services, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative forward returns in over 60% of cases, or interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, increases in industrial production signal restoring capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during recovery phases, or IC drops below zero in post-crisis windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect increased consumer confidence and construction activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months following housing start increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 3.5%, expectations of monetary tightening increase, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in total nonfarm payrolls and a rise in the unemployment rate confirms broad labor market deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Combined labor weakness fails to predict negative returns, with positive outcomes exceeding 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, rising real GDP growth accelerates corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals produce negative forward returns during late-cycle phases, or predictive accuracy falls below chance levels.
